medications.indication_rationale: Used for its anti-inflammatory effect in HS (extended course), not merely as an anti-infective.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g., 100 mg twice daily for ~4 weeks (as per source note; per prescriber)

medications.drug_name: analgesia (regular paracetamol ± NSAID; short strong analgesia for the acute crisis)
medications.indication_rationale: Severe pain crisis needs effective analgesia; short opioid only in-person/prescriber-led.

medications.drug_name: intralesional corticosteroid to a dominant nodule (in person)
medications.indication_rationale: Can rapidly settle a dominant inflamed nodule — performed in person, not remotely.

medications.drug_name: blind incision & drainage of the diffuse flare
medications.indication_rationale: Blind I&D of diffuse HS is often inappropriate/ineffective and worsens scarring; targeted procedures are specialist-led.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Offer smoking-cessation support — smoking is a major driver of HS severity.
Use absorbent non-adherent foam dressings and loose clothing to reduce friction and manage exudate.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces the mechanical trigger and improves comfort/odour.

patient_education_points.point: This is hidradenitis suppurativa — a chronic inflammatory condition of the hair follicles in friction areas, not an infection from poor hygiene. The extended antibiotic is used to calm the inflammation.

Cutting these lumps open usually isn't the answer and can worsen scarring; the plan is anti-inflammatory treatment, dressings, reducing friction, quitting smoking, and a dermatology referral for long-term treatment.
If the redness spreads rapidly down your arm/chest, or you get a high fever with shakes, or the pain becomes intolerable — go to the emergency department.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Pain control, response to anti-inflammatory antibiotics, need for intralesional steroid, and long-term immunomodulatory planning.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Spreading cellulitis, high fever/rigors, or intolerable pain — emergency care.

must_include_items: Recognise HS (chronic sinus tracts/scarring/double-comedones) rather than a simple abscess
Avoid inappropriate blind incision & drainage of diffuse disease
Analgesia + systemic anti-inflammatory antibiotics + absorbent dressings/friction reduction + smoking cessation
Dermatology referral and a cellulitis/sepsis safety-net
acceptable_alternatives: Intralesional corticosteroid for a dominant nodule (in person)
errors_of_commission: Advising/performing blind I&D of diffuse HS
Treating as a simple infection with a short antibiotic course only
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires HS-specific management (anti-inflammatory antibiotics + referral + trigger management), avoidance of inappropriate blind I&D, and a cellulitis/sepsis safety-net.
